requirements.text: WHEN user opens the Project Edit form for a project, the system shall display a color picker dropdown containing 16 preset accent colors
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: select one accent from 16 presets

requirements.text: WHEN user selects a preset accent color in the Project Edit form, the system shall store the selected accent as the current user's preference for that project
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: accent selection saved as user preference

requirements.text: WHEN user enters a custom hex color value in the Project Edit form, the system shall validate the hex value and store it as the current user's preference for that project
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: custom hex color entry

requirements.text: WHEN user enters an invalid custom hex value in the Project Edit form, the system shall display a field-level validation error and shall not replace the previous valid accent
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: invalid hex shows validation error

requirements.text: WHEN user views the Project Edit form, the system shall display a 'choose color' link that opens https://share.google/ATp6ypatbFk69dC91 in a new browser tab
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: choose color link

requirements.text: WHEN user saves a project accent selection, the system shall persist it as a per-user, per-project preference in the owner-owned selector state storage
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: saved as current user's preference

requirements.text: WHEN user saves a project accent selection, the system shall not write the accent to .mdt-config.toml, global project registry, shared project metadata, CLI config, or MCP-visible project output
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: not visible to other users; §2 Constraints

requirements.text: WHEN user A has configured an accent for project X, user B's view of project X shall not reflect user A's accent selection
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: not visible to other users unless independently chosen

requirements.text: WHEN project has a user-configured accent, the system shall display the accent as a compact identity mark on the corresponding project selector chip in the rail
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: selector rail displays configured accent

requirements.text: WHEN project does not have a user-configured accent, the system shall display a deterministic fallback accent on the corresponding selector chip, stable across sessions
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: deterministic fallback accents

requirements.text: WHEN accent identity marks are rendered on selector chips, the system shall not increase the compact chip dimensions
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Non-Functional: no layout shift in selector rail

requirements.text: WHEN project has a user-configured accent, the system shall display the accent as a filled identity area on the corresponding project browser card
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: browser cards display configured accents

requirements.text: WHEN accent identity is rendered on browser cards, the system shall not increase the normal card row height
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: without increasing card row height

requirements.text: WHEN browser card identity area supports both accent-color fill and image fill, the system shall preserve card dimensions regardless of fill type
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: filled identity treatment supports both

requirements.text: WHEN project has no user-configured accent, the system shall assign a deterministic fallback accent derived from the project code, stable across sessions and without requiring migration
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: deterministic fallback; §2 Constraints: deterministic across sessions

requirements.text: WHEN user subsequently configures an accent for a project that previously used a fallback, the system shall replace the fallback with the user-selected accent
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Non-Functional: no reconfigure needed before using selector

requirements.text: WHEN user switches between light and dark mode, the system shall derive theme-appropriate rendering from the same stored accent selection without requiring separate color choices
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: light and dark mode derive from same accent; §2 Constraints: derive from stored accent

requirements.text: WHEN accent marks are rendered in either light or dark mode, the system shall maintain readable foreground text contrast for project code or initials
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Non-Functional: readable contrast in light and dark

requirements.text: WHEN accent identity marks are added to selector chips and browser cards, the system shall preserve existing project search, selection, favorites, and ordering behavior
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: existing behavior continues to work

requirements.text: WHEN accent identity marks are added to browser cards, the system shall preserve existing keyboard navigation and focus management behavior
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §2 Constraints: preserve keyboard navigation

requirements.text: WHEN user submits a custom hex accent value, the backend shall validate the hex format before persisting and shall reject malformed, unsafe, transparent, or unreadable values
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: backend validates custom hex

requirements.text: Accent selections shall be stored in owner-owned project-selector.json only, never in .mdt-config.toml, global project registry, shared project metadata, CLI config, or MCP-visible output
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §2 Constraints: persistence boundary

requirements.text: A single stored hex accent value shall produce theme-appropriate rendering in both light and dark modes without requiring separate per-theme stored colors
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §2 Constraints: derive light and dark from stored accent

requirements.text: Accent identity marks shall not increase the dimensions of selector chips or browser cards
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Non-Functional: no layout shift

requirements.text: The 16-color preset palette shall meet WCAG AA 4.5:1 contrast ratio for foreground text (project code/initials) rendered on accent backgrounds in both light and dark modes
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Non-Functional: readable contrast

requirements.text: Custom hex values shall trigger automatic foreground color selection (light or dark text) based on the accent luminance to maintain readable contrast
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Non-Functional: custom hex constrained

requirements.text: Fallback accent assignment shall be deterministic based on project code, stable across sessions, and shall not require migration before rendering
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §2 Constraints: deterministic fallback

requirements.text: The Project Edit form shall clearly separate personal accent preference controls from shared project metadata fields
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §2 Constraints: not shared project metadata

requirements.text: Backend hex validation shall accept 6-digit hex values with leading # character only and shall reject 3-digit shorthand, non-hex characters, missing #, and transparent values
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §2 Constraints: validation

requirements.text: The 'choose color' external link shall use target=_blank with appropriate security attributes (rel=noopener or equivalent)
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Functional: choose color link opens in new tab

requirements.text: WHEN current user has no configured accent for a project, the system shall render the deterministic fallback accent without error
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Edge: no configured accent

requirements.text: WHEN current user enters a malformed custom hex value, the system shall show a field-level error and preserve the previous valid accent
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Edge: malformed hex

requirements.text: WHEN current user opens the external color helper link and returns without changing the value, the system shall leave the accent unchanged
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Edge: returns without changing

requirements.text: WHEN multiple projects are configured with the same accent color, the system shall render them correctly without conflict
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Edge: same accent for multiple projects

requirements.text: WHEN project has a long code or long name, the accent identity mark shall not affect text truncation or card layout
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Edge: long code/name

requirements.text: WHEN user switches between light and dark mode, accent rendering shall update immediately without page reload or re-selection
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Edge: mode switch

requirements.text: WHEN project has no uploaded image or image fails to load, the accent color shall serve as the sole identity source without degradation
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Edge: no image or image fails

requirements.text: WHEN project folder contains possible logo or image files but the user has not selected one, the system shall not auto-discover or auto-display those files as identity
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Edge: project folder images not selected

requirements.text: WHEN read-only visitor accesses a project, the system shall not write shared project configuration for accent preferences and shall display the deterministic fallback accent for that project
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Edge: read-only visitor

requirements.text: WHEN previously selected personal image is missing from user-owned storage, the system shall fall back to accent color identity — DEFERRED: personal image storage is out of scope for this CR
requirements.sourceRef: MDT-181 §4 Edge: previously selected personal image missing

scenarios.title: User selects a preset accent color from the Project Edit form
scenarios.given: the user opens the Project Edit form for a project
scenarios.when: the user selects one of the 16 preset accent colors from the color picker dropdown
scenarios.then: the selected accent is stored as the current user's preference for that project

scenarios.title: User enters a valid custom hex accent color
scenarios.given: the user opens the Project Edit form for a project
scenarios.when: the user enters a valid custom hex color value (e.g. #e74c3c) in the custom hex input
scenarios.then: the system validates the hex value and stores it as the current user's preference for that project

scenarios.title: Accent selection persists as personal preference without writing shared project config
scenarios.given: the user selects an accent color for a project in the Project Edit form
scenarios.when: the user saves the accent selection
scenarios.then: the accent is persisted in the owner-owned selector state and is not written to .mdt-config.toml, global project registry, shared project metadata, CLI config, or MCP-visible output

scenarios.title: Choose color link opens external color helper in new tab
scenarios.given: the user opens the Project Edit form for a project
scenarios.when: the user clicks the 'choose color' link
scenarios.then: the system opens https://share.google/ATp6ypatbFk69dC91 in a new browser tab

scenarios.title: Selector rail chip displays configured accent as compact identity mark
scenarios.given: the user has configured an accent color for a project
scenarios.when: the project selector rail renders the project chip
scenarios.then: the chip displays the configured accent as a compact identity mark without increasing chip dimensions

scenarios.title: Light and dark mode derive rendering from the same stored accent with readable contrast
scenarios.given: the user has configured a single accent color for a project
scenarios.when: the user switches between light and dark mode
scenarios.then: the system derives theme-appropriate rendering from the same stored accent without requiring separate color choices and maintains readable foreground text contrast for project code or initials

scenarios.title: Accent identity marks do not break existing search, selection, favorites, ordering, or keyboard navigation
scenarios.given: accent identity marks are rendered on selector chips and browser cards
scenarios.when: the user searches projects, selects a project, toggles favorites, or uses keyboard navigation in the browser panel
scenarios.then: existing project search, selection, favorites, ordering behavior and keyboard focus management continue to work as before

scenarios.title: Each user's accent preference is independent of other users
scenarios.given: user A has configured a blue accent for project X
scenarios.when: user B views the same project X with their own green accent configured
scenarios.then: user B sees the green accent and user A's blue accent does not appear in user B's view

scenarios.title: User enters an invalid custom hex value and previous accent is preserved
scenarios.given: the user has previously saved a valid accent for a project and opens the Project Edit form
scenarios.when: the user enters an invalid custom hex value (e.g. 'blue' (named color), '#f00' (3-digit shorthand), '3b82f6' (missing #), '#ffffff00' (transparent))
scenarios.then: the system displays a field-level validation error and the previous valid accent remains unchanged

scenarios.title: Backend rejects malformed custom hex accent values before persistence
scenarios.given: the user submits a custom hex accent value through the API
scenarios.when: the hex value is one of: named color (e.g. 'blue'), 3-digit shorthand (e.g. '#f00'), missing leading # (e.g. '3b82f6'), or transparent (e.g. '#ffffff00')
scenarios.then: the backend rejects the value before persisting and returns a validation error

scenarios.title: Browser card displays filled accent identity area without increasing row height
scenarios.given: the user has configured an accent color for a project
scenarios.when: the project browser panel renders the project card
scenarios.then: the card displays the accent as a filled identity area on the left side preserving the normal card row height. Image fill support is a deferred extensibility point — not tested in this scenario.

scenarios.title: Unconfigured project receives deterministic fallback accent on selector rail
scenarios.given: the user has not configured an accent for a project
scenarios.when: the project selector rail renders the project chip
scenarios.then: the project receives a deterministic fallback accent derived from the project code

scenarios.title: Fallback accent remains stable across page reloads and sessions
scenarios.given: the user has not configured an accent for a project and has seen the fallback accent on the selector rail
scenarios.when: the user reloads the page or opens a new session
scenarios.then: the same deterministic fallback accent appears for that project

scenarios.title: User-configured accent replaces the fallback
scenarios.given: a project is displaying a deterministic fallback accent on the selector rail
scenarios.when: the user configures a personal accent color for that project
scenarios.then: the selector rail and browser panel replace the fallback with the user-selected accent

obligations.title: Extend SelectorStateEntrySchema with optional accent field (hex string) for per-project color preference
Accent hex validation accepts only #RRGGBB format; rejects 3-digit shorthand, named colors, missing #, transparent, and non-hex characters — enforced in both domain-contracts schema and backend API
Define 16-color preset palette with names, hex values, and verified WCAG AA 4.5:1 contrast for foreground text on accent backgrounds in both light and dark modes
Implement automatic foreground color selection (light/dark text) from accent hex luminance using WCAG luminance contrast algorithm — applies to both preset and custom hex values
Implement deterministic getFallbackAccent(projectCode) using project code hash modulo palette size — stable across sessions, no migration required

Single stored hex drives theme-appropriate rendering in both light and dark modes via HSL lightness shift — light mode: lighter surface tints from accent; dark mode: darker surface tints with brighter accent marks. No separate per-theme stored colors.
obligations.sourceRef: assess.md Mismatch: Theme Derivation Strategy; architecture: Theme Derivation

obligations.title: Extend useSelectorData hook to expose setAccent(projectKey, accent) with persistence through /api/config/selector — accent saved independently of shared project metadata update
obligations.sourceRef: assess.md Mismatch: Selector State Schema Extension; architecture: Hook Extension

obligations.title: Add visually separated 'Your Project Accent' section in AddProjectModal edit mode with color picker dropdown (16 presets + custom hex), persisted via /api/config/selector independently of shared project PUT. Section clearly demarcated from shared project metadata fields.
obligations.sourceRef: assess.md Mismatch: Project Edit Form — Shared vs Personal Boundary; architecture: Edit Form Section

obligations.title: Frontend inline field-level validation for custom hex input — rejects invalid values without overriding the previous valid accent. Uses same #RRGGBB validation rules as backend.
Choose color link uses target=_blank with rel=noopener noreferrer — opens https://share.google/ATp6ypatbFk69dC91 in new tab with security attributes
Project selector chips render accent as a compact identity mark (colored indicator bar, dot, or border tint) via inline CSS custom property — does not increase chip dimensions
obligations.sourceRef: assess.md Mismatch: Card/Chip Identity Rendering; architecture: Chip Accent

obligations.title: Canonical accent resolution flow: read selectorState.accent → if present use it; if absent call getFallbackAccent(projectCode) → pass resolved accent to chip/card components via inline style CSS custom property

Accent persistence goes through /api/config/selector only — never written to .mdt-config.toml, global project registry, shared project metadata, CLI config, or MCP-visible output. Read-only visitors receive fallback accents only.
Accent identity marks must preserve existing project search, selection, favorites, ordering, keyboard navigation, and focus management behavior — additive change only

CSS system uses per-element inline CSS custom property (style=--project-accent: #hex) for accent application. Theme adaptation via CSS opacity/overlay technique or HSL-derived surface variants defined in accent utilities. No static CSS variable per accent.
obligations.sourceRef: assess.md Mismatch: Theme Derivation Strategy; architecture: CSS Variable System

obligations.title: Extend existing test suites (domain-contracts accent schema, useSelectorData accent read/write, selector API accent validation, browser panel accent rendering) plus new accent utilities unit tests for palette, fallback, foreground, hex validation
obligations.sourceRef: assess.md: Verification Gaps; architecture: Test Coverage

obligations.title: Project browser cards render accent as a filled identity area on the left side with slot-based design supporting both accent-color fill and future image fill — preserves card row height regardless of fill type
obligations.sourceRef: assess.md Mismatch: Card/Chip Identity Rendering; architecture: Card Accent Identity
